topics.summary: A ruling Democratic Party-led constitutional amendment bill is scheduled for a National Assembly plenary vote on May 7, aimed at holding a national referendum concurrent with the June 3 local elections. The PPP is maintaining party-line opposition and threatening to boycott the vote entirely, making the required two-thirds majority of 191 seats nearly impossible to reach.

evidence_headlines.title: 개헌안 7일 본회의 표결… '윤석열 방지법' vs '독재적 발상' 대치 [이런정치]
개헌 본회의 D-3…국힘 표결 참석 여부 촉각(종합)
Speaker Woo Rallies Six Parties for Constitutional Amendment Push

topics.topic_en: June 3 Local Elections Final Campaign Push
topics.summary: With exactly one month until the June 3 local and by-elections, both parties have finalized candidate lineups: the ruling DPK is deploying presidential aides and heavyweights while the PPP fields incumbents. Polls show the DPK strongly favored, with Gyeonggi Province's semiconductor belt emerging as a key battleground district.

evidence_headlines.title: June 3 elections take shape: DPK's presidential aides vs. PPP's incumbents
Elections are a month away. What's at stake?
Gyeonggi's chip belt becomes key battleground in June elections

topics.topic_en: PPP Leadership Discord Ahead of Elections
topics.topic_ko: 국민의힘 지도부 분열 및 후보 이탈
topics.summary: PPP chairman Jang Dong-hyuk and floor leader Song Eon-seok are conducting separate campaign activities ahead of the June 3 vote, with local candidates openly distancing themselves from central party leadership. Internal disagreements over candidate nominations and strategic direction continue to hamper the opposition's electoral cohesion.

evidence_headlines.title: Divided PPP Leadership Campaigns Separately as Candidates Go Solo
PPP candidates keep distance from party leadership ahead of June 3 elections
People Power Party Names Single Candidates in 7 Districts for By-Elections

topics.topic_en: Bank of Korea Signals Imminent Rate Hike
topics.topic_ko: 한국은행 금리 인상 신호 및 물가 압력
topics.summary: Bank of Korea Senior Deputy Governor Ryoo Sang-dae said on May 4 that it is "time to consider raising rates," marking a decisive hawkish shift. Markets now price in two hikes in 2026 lifting the benchmark to 3%, as oil-driven inflation is tracking above the central bank's prior forecasts.

evidence_headlines.title: Bank of Korea Official Says It's Time to Consider Interest Rate Hike
BOK Signals Shift to Tightening, Two Rate Hikes Seen This Year
South Korea: Oil shock lifts inflation outlook

topics.topic_en: Kim Keon-hee Case Reaches Supreme Court
topics.summary: The Kim Keon-hee special counsel filed an appeal to the Supreme Court on May 4, challenging the appellate court's four-year sentence handed down to former President Yoon's wife for stock manipulation and political funds violations. The Seoul High Court is simultaneously fast-tracking multiple special counsel appeal cases.

evidence_headlines.title: 특검, 김건희 도이치 주가조작 항소심 징역 4년 상고‥"법리 오해"
한국 특검, 김건희 1심 사건 2심 판결에 항소 제기
서울고법 "'3대 특검' 항소심 신속 진행 중"‥다음 주 김건희

topics.summary: Yoon Suk-yeol, sentenced to life imprisonment in February 2026 for leading the December 2024 insurrection, saw a related arrest-obstruction appellate ruling raise his co-defendants' sentences in late April. Multiple insurrection-linked trials remain in active appeals proceedings ahead of the June elections.

evidence_headlines.title: Yoon Suk Yeol: Former South Korean President handed life sentence for leading insurrection
2026년 5월 윤석열 체포방해 항소심 결과·형량·판단 근거 3가지
윤석열 내란 재판

topics.summary: The US-Korea tariff framework deal, reducing auto tariffs from 25% to 15% and unlocking a $350 billion Korean investment commitment, remains contested as semiconductor Section 232 tariff rates are still unresolved. The Lee government continues to navigate Trump administration pressure while seeking to protect key export sectors.

evidence_headlines.title: US, South Korea reach deal lowering auto tariffs and unlocking $350 billion investment
South Korea-U.S. tariff strife threatens to affect security domain
U.S.-South Korea Relations in 2026: Key Issues to Watch

topics.topic_en: Judicial Reform Laws Spark Ongoing Controversy
topics.summary: Three landmark judicial reform laws enacted in March 2026—expanding the Supreme Court to 26 justices, introducing a constitutional complaint mechanism, and criminalizing judicial distortion—continue to generate controversy, including a criminal complaint against the Supreme Court Chief Justice and opposition warnings about executive capture of the judiciary.

evidence_headlines.title: New law lets president pick 22 of 26 Supreme Court justices by 2030
Supreme Court Chief Justice Faces Criminal Complaint on First Day of Judicial Reform Laws
South Korea Enacts Judicial Reform Laws Amid 'Endless Retrial' Concerns

method_notes: Captured via Anthropic API (Sonnet 4.6) with web_search for live Korean political coverage. Topic labels (topic_en) are stable handles for cross-snapshot rank comparison.
